summary: Implemented validation middleware for URL path parameters and error handling middleware for Gin. ValidateSlug enforces ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9-]*[a-zA-Z0-9]$ regex on :slug params. ValidateTaskID enforces ^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$ and blocks path traversal (..). ErrorHandler catches scanner model errors and maps them to consistent JSON error responses with appropriate HTTP status codes (400, 404, 500).
keyDecisions: Regex patterns compiled at package init time (var declarations) rather than per-request for performance
Task ID validation uses regex match plus explicit path traversal check for '..' since dots are valid in task IDs (e.g., '1.1') but '..' is not
Error handler is post-handler middleware (uses c.Next() then checks c.Errors) to catch errors from downstream handlers
Internal error types (ErrInvalidSlug) map to external codes (invalid_slug) in the error handler middleware, keeping model layer clean
Gin's router naturally rejects path params containing '/' so middleware only needs to handle non-slash invalid chars

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: internal/handler/middleware.go implements validation middleware for :id, :slug, :taskId path params
Task ID validation: ^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$ (no path traversal sequences like ..)
Invalid input returns HTTP 400 with {"error": "invalid_slug", "message": "..."} or {"error": "invalid_task_id", ...}
Gin error middleware catches scanner errors and maps to appropriate HTTP status codes (404, 500)
Unit tests: valid slug passes, ../ rejected, spaces rejected, valid task IDs pass, .. rejected

notes: The slug regex in the implementation uses [a-zA-Z0-9] (case-insensitive for letters) rather than [a-z0-9] as stated in the task file, because the tech-design.md uses ^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$ as the regex pattern. Both achieve the same goal of alphanumeric + hyphens.
